summary: Breakpoint in `_get_message` of class inheriting from `Translation` crashes on startup

description.text:
### Tested versions

Not reproducible in: v4.0.3.stable.official [5222a99f5], v4.1.4.stable.official [fe0e8e557], v4.2.dev1.official [0c2144da9]

Reproducible in: v4.2.dev2.official [da81ca62a], v4.2.2.stable.official [15073afe3], v4.3.stable.official [77dcf97d8], v4.4.rc3.official [15ff45068]

### System information

Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3070 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6636) - AMD Ryzen 9 7900X 12-Core Processor (24 threads)

### Issue description

When attempting to set a breakpoint in the `_get_message` function of a subclass of `Translation`, Godot crashes immediately on starting the project. Either the `breakpoint` keyword or a breakpoint set in the editor will cause the crash.

Likely the same issue as #91524. It being related to engine debugging features might explain the other user's problem going away in a new project. The MRP has the subclass in the `main.gd` file for brevity, but the issue exists whether it's there or in a separate file.

I've narrowed it down to being introduced somewhere between v4.2.dev1 and v4.2.dev2, and the issue persists all the way up to the most recent v4.4.rc3.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Run the provided MRP in any of the affected versions. -> Godot crashes.
2. Remove/comment out the `breakpoint` line in the `CustomTL` class in `main.gd` and run the project again. -> Godot does not crash.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[translation-bug.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19042485/translation-bug.zip)

summary: AnimationPlayer saving unset visualshader floatparameter as 'null' instead of '0'.

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in 4.3.stable

### System information

Windows 10 - 

### Issue description

When creating a new track in AnimationPlayer for a visualshader's parameters, if a value has not been explicitly given a default, or the user has not typed the value '0' first, then the default value, despite displaying as zero, is actually saved as null. this prevents the track from actually animating to or from this number, but the value still displays as 'zero' in editor. This can be worked around if a user knows what's happened, but is not intuitive. It also does not happen with a shader, only visualshader.

### Steps to reproduce

Create a sprite2d or texturerect
Create a new shader material
Create and attach a new visual shader
In that visualshader, create a floatparameter and attach it to color.

Create an animationplayer
Create a new animation
Locate the created visualshader parameter, you may create a range, but do not put in a default value.
Click the keyframe button before setting a value of any type.

If you now create a second keyframe of any value elsewhere on the timeline, it will not animate between them, because the value is null, and this can be confirmed by looking at the raw text of the saved scene.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[minimum_project.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19041863/minimum_project.zip)

summary: Forced window min size doesn't make sense

description.text:
### Tested versions

4.4.rc3

### System information

Godot v4.4.rc3 - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3060 Ti (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6636) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-6700K CPU @ 4.00GHz (8 threads)

### Issue description

I'm trying to implement proper window management, which will allow changing window mode and monitor, and remember window positions which are unrelated to fullscreen mode. For example, if your window is on monitor 0, and you want to make it fullscreen on monitor 1, currently in Godot you have to first move the window to the monitor 1, then set the fullscreen mode. Then when you set the window back to windowed mode, it first becomes windowed on monitor 1, and only then you can move it back to monitor 0. This might show some window jumping. To aleviate this issue, one could first make this window borderless, make it size 1,1, move it into the corner of monitor 1, and make it fullscreen.

So here's the thing, in project settings user can set window size override to 1,1 -- and this will initially create a window with size 1,1. However, later in the initialization process Godot forces min size to 64,64 (due to some old issue #37242). However, this constraint is not enforced, and user can just call `window_ set_min_width/height(1,1)` and this will actually set it.

This initial forced 64,64 min size is pretty inconsistent in this regard, and should be a configurable setting with 64,64 default being a recommendation. Min size 1,1 doesn't break most projects, so it doesn't make sense to restrict every project.

### Steps to reproduce

N/A

N/A

summary: Set editor's translation domain at root node

description.text:
@KoBeWi [found](https://chat.godotengine.org/channel/editor?msg=WyHwYBGXxsQbxAc7J) that some strings in the save file confirm dialog are not translated (e.g. window title):

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/906edaa8-92be-46c9-96e5-b4f1a153f745)

This is because the dialog has recently been given the possibility to be reparented to the root node. However, the translation domain of the editor only takes effect on the `EditorNode` node, so auto translation won't work.

https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/15ff450680a40391aabbffde0a57ead2cd84db56/editor/editor_node.cpp#L5750-L5754

Changing the translation domain to be set at the root node can also help to resolve other potential similar issues as well.

*Bugsquad edit:* Fixes #103321

summary: Animation Tree infinite memory usage when loading a state machine into itself

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in both 4.3 and 4.4-rc3

### System information

Godot v4.3.stable - Windows 10.0.19045 - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 2060 SUPER (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6590) - 12th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i5-12600K (16 Threads)

### Issue description

If you have an animation tree with a State Machine inside of it, and with a reference to that State Machine saved to disk as a .tres file, attempting to load that resource from disk will immediately freeze the editor and cause it to take up as much memory as is allowed (On my computer it jumped from <1gb to up to 22gb).


### Steps to reproduce

I would recommend having Task Manager or something similar open when doing this

1. Set AnimationTree root node to AnimationNodeStateMachine
2. Save that root node to disk as a resource (I called mine `top_level_machine.tres`)
3. Change root node to AnimationNodeBlendTree
4. Right click -> Load -> Select `top_level_machine.tres`
5. Click "Open Editor" on the state machine
6. Right click -> Load -> Select `top_level_machine.tres`

If using Minimal reproduction project, you only need to do steps 5 and 6

summary: On Linux, some default terminals for FileSystem dock's "Open in Terminal" are not invoked correctly

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in current 4.4.rc master (15ff450)
- Reproducible since "open in terminal" was added

### System information

Godot v4.4.rc (ad4a2cdde) - Arch Linux #1 SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Sat, 22 Feb 2025 00:37:05 +0000 on X11 - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4070 (nvidia; 570.86.16) - AMD Ryzen 9 9950X 16-Core Processor (32 threads)

### Issue description

**What happens**

After selecting "Open in Terminal" for a file or folder in the FileSystem dock, an error is printed to Godot's output, but nothing else happens.

**What should happen**

A terminal should open with the folder, or the folder the file is in, as its working directory. Or, at least, an error should be reported to the user.

**Why it happens**

In `editor/filesystem_dock.cpp` around line 2252, the right arguments are chosen for passing to the terminal to make it change to the directory. This is only done for konsole, gnome-terminal, urxvt, and xfce4-terminal.

This means other Linux/BSD terminals are called with the bare directory name as the argument, which only works for Kitty.

**Notes on a fix**

It's easily fixed for alacritty because it supports the same `--working-directory` argument as many other terminals.

lxterminal supports `--working-directory=dir`, but _not_ `--working-directory dir`.

xterm does not support an argument for specifying a working directory.

**Workaround**

Specifying `filesystem/external_programs/terminal_emulator_flags` is a straightforward workaround, but many users might not think to do that, and no error message is displayed to suggest it.

### Steps to reproduce

On a system where one of lxterminal, alacritty, or xterm will be selected as the terminal emulator.

(Note: Godot will select the first it can find out of gnome-terminal, konsole, xfce4-terminal, lxterminal, kitty, alacritty, urvxt, and xterm.)

Create a fresh project, right-click icon.svg in the FileSystem dock, and choose "Open Folder in Terminal". An error - from the terminal application - will be printed to Godot's output. Nothing else will happen.

N/A

summary: Allow Fullscreen when Embed Game View

description.text:

### System information

Solus 4.7 Endurance Gnome

### Issue description

In Godot 4.4.beta 1 it was possible to test the game embed in the editor even if the game was set to fullscreen.

User experience-wise, if I'm playing the game embed, I don't want to play it fullscreen. If I want to test the game full screen I could test the game with play embed disabled.

This allowed me to make quick, efficient, straight forward tests and record fullscreen footage with the click of a button [enable/disable embed].

With the current approach, that I don't understand why this changed happened, there's a lot of attriction to make what in 4.4.beta1 was smooth.

Please, allow us to keep the Window Mode set to Fullscreen AND when playing embed, it just adapts the window size to the editor's viewport.

I know there might be a good reason for that, but it was a massive downgrade in UX.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Download 4.4.beta1
2. Create a game project
3. Open Project -> Project Settings -> Window
4. Set the Mode property to "Fullscreen"
5. In the "Game" view, enable "Embed Game on Next Play"
6. Test the game and see it perfectly working in the editor's viewport on the Game view
7. Disable the "Embed Game on Next Play" option
8. Test the game and it will open in fullscreen, perfectly
---
1. Download 4.4.beta3 or newer
2. Repeat steps 2-4
3. Godot will prevent you from reproducing step 5
4. Now every time you want to test the game embed you will need to do the following
    1. Open Project -> Project Settings -> Window
    2. Set the Mode property to "Window"
    3. In the "Game" view, enable "Embed Game on Next Play"
5. And if you want to test the game fullscreen, you will need to repeat steps 3, 4, and 7 instead of just 7

summary: Allow attaching scripts to nodes in the Advanced Import Settings dialog

description.text:
The Blender Project DogWalk team has been using scenes with an imported glTF scene as a child node in order to attach scripts to the nodes and save that. This is a good use case for inherited scenes, and the editable children checkbox, but there are currently some issues with those.

Regardless of improving imported scenes, being able to attach scripts to nodes in imported scenes within the import process itself is a highly useful feature. We already allow customizing mesh nodes with common things like physics, but other nodes are missing customizability. For many things like physics, ideally users would use glTF extensions. However, there will always be a need for custom game-specific scripts that do not make sense to store in the glTF at all.

This PR adds the ability to use the Advanced Import Settings dialog to attach scripts to nodes so they are available when directly dragging the glTF into the scene. Here's a video showcasing using this feature. The node type will automatically change to accommodate the script if needed, or will use the user-specified type when possible.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c8c7d735-7f1b-45a9-b95f-ba23c281b7a5

Essentially, for any node type or script that can't be determined in the green circle, this PR allows attaching the script or setting node type in the orange circle, instead of using an inherited scene or other scene to attach the script. Also, in the red part, you can't change the type of an existing node if the script would require a different base Godot node type.

![attach_scripts](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8bf18c33-3297-4088-9c88-8751be0d8878)

Small change to add a button which creates a new AnimationPlayer node to the animation track editor when there is no AnimationPlayer node selected. This uses similar logic to `SceneTreeDock::_do_create`. I think it would be worth following up with a similar change to create a new animation when there are none in the AnimationPlayer, and potentially selecting an AnimationPlayer node automatically when the pane is opened if only one is present in the scene (both of which I'm hoping to look at). Altogether this should make the animation workflow quite easily discoverable and intuitive.

summary: Stuff Added Via RenderingServer Inside CompositorEffect Are Duplicated On C# Rebuild in Editor

description.text:
### Tested versions

v4.4.dev7.mono.official [46c8f8c5c]

### System information

Godot v4.4.dev7.mono - Ubuntu 24.04.2 LTS 24.04 on X11 - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 2070 SUPER (nvidia; 550.120) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i5-9400F CPU @ 2.90GHz (6 threads)

### Issue description

**Disclaimer:** I'm not sure this is a bug per se, or just a lack of documentation, or a misunderstanding of the proper way to do things...

I'm working on a `CompositorEffect`. In my `RS.CallOnRenderThread(new Callable(this, MethodName._InitializeCompute))` method I'm creating some stuff in the rendering server _(i.e. `RS.MeshCreate()`, `RS.InstanceCreate()`, etc)_ that I need for the effect I'm making. It works, however, in the editor I'm noticing that whenever I need to rebuild the C# project, I'm getting duplicates of the things I created _(like, in the case of a mesh, I then have two meshes after rebuild)_.

I'm not sure if this is strictly limited to `CompositorEffect` or not. It could be that it is simply related to *anything* that is initialized on the `RenderingServer` and not cleaned up before a rebuild. It could be the fact that there doesn't seem to be anything that is exposed to `CompositorEffect` that is called before a rebuild happens, like a destructor.

I assume this is a C#-specific issue because GDScript doesn't have the rebuild step like C# does. I also assume this is an editor-specific issue since you don't rebuild the project in the final exported game.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Open the MRP in Godot.
2. Click on `Build Project`.
3. You should see 5 red squares added to the scene.
4. Edit `CompositorTest.cs` _(like just add a comment or something)_.
5. Go back to the editor and click on `Build Project` again.
6. You will see that 5 more red squares will be added, instead of the original 5 being replaced.

summary: Mbed TLS error when exporting with PCK encryption (musl libc)

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: 4.4-beta1 and later
- Not reproducible in: 4.3-stable

### System information

Alpine Linux - musl libc - gcc - Godot 4.4.rc2 - AMD GPU

### Issue description

I have compiled both the editor and the export templates in my system (Alpine Linux with musl libc and GCC) as usual, with my encryption key exported.
When I try to export the game with the PCK encryption enabled, fails at 20% with the following errors:

```
ERROR:  failed
  ! mbedtls_ctr_drbg_seed returned an error -52.
   at: init (core/crypto/crypto_core.cpp:73)
ERROR: Failed to initialize random number generator.
   at: open_and_parse (core/io/file_access_encrypted.cpp:52)
ERROR: Condition "err != OK" is true. Returning: ERR_SKIP
   at: _save_pack_file (editor/export/editor_export_platform.cpp:304)
ERROR: Save PCK: Failed to export project files.
   at: add_message (editor/export/editor_export_platform.h:243)
```

It works OK if I use the same compiled templates in the official 4.4-rc2 release running on my steam deck (GLIBC), so I think that the issue is in my compiled editor, not in the templates.

### Steps to reproduce

I've compiled the editor and templates with the following commands under Alpine Linux (musl libc) using GCC:

```
# Editor
```

Shift left and right already do something, which is expanding and collapsing recursively. So this only adds shift up and down

summary: Disabling "Embed Subwindows" breaks manual input sent via `get_viewport().push_input()` in Godot 4.4

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in Godot 4.4-beta1 and later
- Not reproducible in Godot 4.3-stable and earlier

### System information

Tested on Arch Linux (KDE) and Windows 11

### Issue description

I manually create an `InputEventMouseButton` and push it via `get_viewport().push_input` to press a GUI button. The button is pressed as expected on Godot 4.3 and earlier, as well as on Godot 4.4 as long as the "Embed Subwindows" option is enabled in the project settings.

However, when "Embed Subwindows" is disabled, the behavior is strange since Godot 4.4: the button does not react to the pushed input, even though the "Misc" panel in the Debugger correctly identifies the last clicked control as the Button.

The button neither reacts visually nor does it emit signals in this case.

### Steps to reproduce

We manually create mouse clicks like this:

```gdscript
var event = InputEventMouseButton.new()
event.pressed = true
event.button_index = 1
event.position = Vector2(200, 50)
event.global_position = Vector2(200, 50)

get_viewport().push_input(event, false)

await get_tree().process_frame

# Send a mouse release event immediately after
var release_event = event.duplicate()
release_event.pressed = false

get_viewport().push_input(release_event, false)
```

The position must correspond to the location of a UI element. With "Embed Subwindows", the UI node at the corresponding location is pressed as expected by this code. But without "Embed Subwindows", the UI does not react to the manually created event (even though it does react to a normal mouse click).

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[viewport-input-test.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19011128/viewport-input-test.zip)

Pressing the "hallo" button manually prints "hallo".
Pressing the "press the other button" button causes the "hallo" button not to be pressed, even though it should be. When setting `window/subwindows/embed_subwindows` to `true` (either in the project settings GUI or in project.godot), "press the other button" does cause "hallo" to be pressed, printing "hallo" to the output.

summary: [3.x] Dispose override crashes the editor (disposing GameSocket)

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in 3.6 stable (mono)
- Reproducible in 3.5.3 stable (mono)

### System information

Windows 10, GLES2

### Issue description

This little bit of code crashes the editor when **starting up** my program. I did some digging for related issues, such as https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/93287, https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/80834.

But I think these are different issues. In my case, the editor crashes before anything should be disposed (on launch of the game). The actual game does seem to call the code in the dispose function correctly. So the issue is the editor crash.
```
using Godot;

public class Node2D : Godot.Node2D
{
    WebSocketClient GameSocket;

    protected override void Dispose(bool disposing)
    {
        GameSocket.DisconnectFromHost();
        GameSocket.Dispose();
        base.Dispose(disposing);
    }
}
```
I know it's not how you're supposed to use Dispose, but crashing the editor seems like unintended behavior. If anything, I would expect errors in the console. Crashes don't seem 100% deterministic, sometimes a crash happens, sometimes it doesn't. This variation I found to cause crashes very frequently.

In the test project, the crashes seem to be preventable by using the following code, but the editor still crashes occasionally in my main project.

```
protected override void Dispose(bool disposing)
    {
        if (disposing) {
            GameSocket.DisconnectFromHost();
            GameSocket.Dispose();
        }
        base.Dispose(disposing);
    }
```

The engine's console contains the following information

summary: Godot 4.3 Editor (C#, Linux) Goes Unresponding After System Wakes Up From a Suspension

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: 4.3.stable

### System information

Godot v4.3.stable.mono - Ubuntu 22.04.5 LTS 22.04 - X11 - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1050 (nvidia; 535.183.01) - AMD Ryzen 5 2500U with Radeon Vega Mobile Gfx (8 Threads)

### Issue description

When I suspend the system and start it again later, Godot editor becomes unresponding and does not even quit by clicking the exit button. The only way to close it and start it again is by issuing a kill -9 command. It is worth noting that this behaviour only happens when I actually open a project in Godot editor: If no projects are selected, the editor works fine after suspension.

This error keeps being printed when the bug happens:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f87d8d14-cb3e-42e9-8269-a9ade989cf46)

### Steps to reproduce

1. Lunch Godot.
2. Open a project.
3. Suspend the system.
4. Start the system again.
5. Godot is unresponsive (does not work neither will quit). Can only shut it down with a kill -9 command.

summary: Inspector overridden properties
Copy and paste actions might not work on the expected properties when a sub-inspector is open

description.text:
### Tested versions

master [01545c995]
v4.3.stable.official [77dcf97d8]


### System information

Godot v4.3.stable - Linux Mint 22.1 (Xia) - X11 - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1050 Ti (nvidia; 535.183.01) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i5-7300HQ CPU @ 2.50GHz (4 Threads)

### Issue description

Copy and paste actions in inspectors and child inspectors use the same shortcuts. They are performed according to the selection. Mouse clicks do not deselect selections in (parent) child inspectors. So it looks like it will be executed on the last selected item.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8a5a9428-23bf-44c9-896a-16843743305c



### Steps to reproduce

1. Add a `Conrol`;
2. In the inspector, add a `CanvasItemMaterial` to **Material**,
3. Click `CanvasItemMaterial` to expand the sub-inspector. At this time, there is no selected item in the sub-inspector, **Local to Scen**e is not checked, and **Z as Relative** is checked by default;
4. Click **Local to Scene** in the sub-inspector and use `Ctrl + C`;
5. Click **Z as Relative** and use `Ctrl + V`, the value does not change. However, when you open the **Output** panel at the bottom, "Set resource_local_to_scene" will be displayed.

summary: Some right-click popup menu positions may be incorrect

description.text:
### Tested versions

master [01545c995]
v4.3.stable.official [77dcf97d8]

### System information

Godot v4.3.stable - Linux Mint 22.1 (Xia) - X11 - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1050 Ti (nvidia; 535.183.01) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i5-7300HQ CPU @ 2.50GHz (4 Threads)

### Issue description

There are three formulas for calculating the popup position based on the mouse position in controls:
1. `get_screen_transform().xform(get_local_mouse_position())`;
2. `get_screen_position() + get_local_mouse_position()`;
3. `get_screen_position() + mb->get_position()`.


`get_screen_position()` is short for `get_screen_transform().get_origin()`. 

The first formula seems to be the most versatile, but the calculation is slightly more.

If `get_screen_transform()` has only a translation transform (no scale, no skew, no rotation), then the second and first formulas are equal.

We may be able to ensure that the control's transform used in the editor is a translation-only transform, but there is no way to ensure that this is the case for the user, such as #53575. Although in 99% of cases, control's transformations are translation-only transforms.

The position of mouse button events is a cached value, but it's local to `gui.mouse_focus`, if  `this` is `gui.mouse_focus`, then the second and third formulas are equal.:
https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/3ef055fecb16714760a1b7fdaf8b8f8f0a06114d/scene/main/viewport.cpp#L1882-L1883

In `EditorInspectorArray`, it seems that there is a case where `this` is not `gui.mouse_focus`, see:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f0c4717f-d73f-4bf1-a2e9-c55def9a3ec3

https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/3ef055fecb16714760a1b7fdaf8b8f8f0a06114d/editor/editor_inspector.cpp#L2090



### Steps to reproduce

`EditorInspectorArray`:

1. Create a scene;
2. Add an `ItemList`;
3. Add more elements under `Items` in the inspector;
4. Right-click near the leftmost icon of the element to open the pop-up menu.

`LineEdit`, `TextEdit`, `RichTextLabel`, ...

1. Create a scene and add a `LineEdit`:
2. Set **Layout Mode** to **Anchors** in the Inspector;
3. Set **Anchors Preset** to **Custom**;
4. Set **Right** and **Bottom** under **Anchors Points** to `0.2`;
5. Set **Scale** to `(5, 5)`;
6. Run the scene, right-click, and observe the location of the popup menu;
7. In the Scene dock, right-click the configured `LineEdit`, select **Change Type**, change the type to another type, and repeat step 6. Note that some types require some additional configuration.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[wrong-popup-position.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19002210/wrong-popup-position.zip)
